original_procedural:
1. Encountered Toptal and spoke with an expert within a few several hours.
2. Realized the expert was the right person for the project after discussion.
3. Dealt with an application programmer for iOS iPhone and iPad app development.
4. Determined the cost to develop an app varies, estimated at some thousand pounds.
5. Then, considered getting valuable phrases for the project outline.
6. Integrated ideas into the project specification.
7. Noticed that not knowing who to hire could lead to bitter experiences.
8. Worked with Toptal's developers and architects, finding them professional.
9. Developed a solution that was affordable and high-quality, reducing time to launch.
10. Launched the application, ensuring it met the project's scope and goals.
11. Ensured that Core Data objects were handled correctly to avoid errors.
12. Used NSZombies in Xcode to debug exc_bad_access mistakes.
13. Paid fees and costs associated with the platform and development.
14. Invoked solutions by sending messages to objects, such as myUser.
15. Started a demo period of up to 2 months to verify engagement success.
16. Billed for time and continued the engagement after successful verification.
17. Developed and launched the application, meeting the client's needs.
18. Ensured the application was of high quality and met the project's goals.
19. Eventually, achieved a successful outcome with the project.
20. After the project, verified that the application was working as expected.
21. Worked with the developer to ensure the application met the client's needs.
22. Integrated the application with Core Data and other necessary components.
23. Tested the application to ensure it was working correctly.
24. Launched the application and made it available to users.
25. Monitored the application's performance and made necessary updates.
26. Ensured that the application was stable and functioning correctly.
27. Continued to work with the developer to maintain and update the application.
28. Added new features and functionality to the application as needed.
29. Ensured that the application remained compatible with iOS and iPad devices.
30. Updated the application to fix any bugs or issues that arose.
31. After verifying the application's success, continued to maintain and update it.

original_procedural:
1. Said Agus Justianto, performance of sustainable forest management in 2023 exceeded targets.
2. Implemented multi-forestry business model in PBPH, making forest management more inclusive.
3. Said Justianto, 667,780 hectares of PBPH area were planted and enriched in 2023.
4. Reached log production of 58.13 million cubic meters, 101.9 percent of target.
5. Disclosed Justianto, 89.01 percent of log production came from plantation forests.
6. Said Justianto, non-timber forest products production increased to 820.2 thousand tons in 2023.
7. Expanded community access to forest management through forestry partnerships.
8. Realized provision of legal access to 20,643 hectares of land in 2023, 137.6 percent of target.
9. Said Justianto, forestry partnerships increased real economic value of forest areas.
10. Implemented MUK on 14,078 hectares of PBPH areas and 28,203 hectares in KPH units.
11. Said Indroyono Soesilo, business climate is positive, supporting sustainable forest management.
12. Said Soesilo, PBPH will support ministry's sustainable forest management directions.
validated_procedural:
1. Said Agus Justianto, performance of sustainable forest management in 2023 exceeded targets.
2. Implemented multi-forestry business model in PBPH, making forest management more inclusive.
3. Said Justianto, 667,780 hectares of PBPH area were planted and enriched in 2023.
4. Reached log production of 58.13 million cubic meters, 101.9 percent of target.
5. Disclosed Justianto, 89.01 percent of log production came from plantation forests.
6. Said Justianto, non-timber forest products production increased to 820.2 thousand tons in 2023.
7. Expanded community access to forest management through forestry partnerships.
8. Realized provision of legal access to 20,643 hectares of land in 2023, 137.6 percent of target.
9. Said Justianto, forestry partnerships increased real economic value of forest areas.
10. Implemented MUK on 14,078 hectares of PBPH areas and 28,203 hectares in KPH units.
11. Said Indroyono Soesilo, business climate is positive, supporting sustainable forest management.
12. Said Soesilo, PBPH will support ministry's sustainable forest management directions.

original_procedural:
1. Entered into Agreement and Plan of Merger on May 25, 2023
2. Filed putative class action complaint on June 26, 2023
3. Alleged false statements in preliminary proxy statement
4. Sought preliminary injunction to enjoin Merger
5. Filed definitive proxy statement on June 26, 2023
6. Filed amended complaint on June 29, 2023
7. Agreed to make additional disclosures
8. Filed Amendment No. 1 to Proxy on July 10, 2023
9. Dismissed Action as moot on August 1, 2023
10. Entered stipulated order dismissing Action
11. Retained jurisdiction for attorneys' fees application
12. Agreed to pay $114,000 for attorneys' fees
13. Entered order closing case on December 21, 2023
14. Filed affidavit confirming notice issuance subsequently
validated_procedural:
1. Entered into Agreement and Plan of Merger on May 25, 2023
2. Filed putative class action complaint on June 26, 2023
3. Alleged false statements in preliminary proxy statement
4. Sought preliminary injunction to enjoin Merger
5. Filed definitive proxy statement on June 26, 2023
6. Filed amended complaint on June 29, 2023
7. Agreed to make additional disclosures
8. Filed Amendment No. 1 to Proxy on July 10, 2023
9. Dismissed Action as moot on August 1, 2023
10. Entered stipulated order dismissing Action
11. Retained jurisdiction for attorneys' fees application
12. Agreed to pay $114,000 for attorneys' fees
13. Entered order closing case on December 21, 2023
14. Filed affidavit confirming notice issuance subsequently

original_procedural:
1. Completed projects in 2023 and planned for 2024.
2. Developed International Ecological Footprint Learning Lab with York University and University of Iceland.
3. Developed Urban Consumption Guidebook for Cities with C40 Cities and others.
4. Developed BC-specific Policy and Planning Guide with Real Estate Foundation of BC.
5. Completed Ecological Footprint assessments for City of Ottawa and others.
6. Delivered presentations, including Cora Hallsworth to Climate Caucus.
7. Led Ecocity Webinar on Sustainable Lifestyles in May.
8. Participated in Ecocity World Summit in June.
9. Coordinated Ecocity Peer Network Zoom meeting in October.
10. Published paper on sustainability at urban scale with Université du Québec.
11. Interviewed Dr. Jennie Moore on CKNW 980 radio and Vancouver Sun.
12. Developed Ecological Footprint as core metric in Saanich's Official Community Plan.
validated_procedural:
1. Completed projects in 2023 and planned for 2024.
2. Developed International Ecological Footprint Learning Lab with York University and University of Iceland.
3. Developed Urban Consumption Guidebook for Cities with C40 Cities and others.
4. Developed BC-specific Policy and Planning Guide with Real Estate Foundation of BC.
5. Completed Ecological Footprint assessments for City of Ottawa and others.
6. Delivered presentations, including Cora Hallsworth to Climate Caucus.
7. Led Ecocity Webinar on Sustainable Lifestyles in May.
8. Participated in Ecocity World Summit in June.
9. Coordinated Ecocity Peer Network Zoom meeting in October.
10. Published paper on sustainability at urban scale with Université du Québec.
11. Interviewed Dr. Jennie Moore on CKNW 980 radio and Vancouver Sun.
12. Developed Ecological Footprint as core metric in Saanich's Official Community Plan.

original_procedural:
1. Revealed a research paper on optimal table size for jigsaw puzzles.
2. Completed nine jigsaws with 9-2,000 pieces, Dr Madeleine Bonsma-Fisher and Dr Kent Bonsma-Fisher.
3. Measured puzzle pieces, laying them out naturally.
4. Completed each jigsaw, calculating assembled picture dimensions.
5. Compared theory and data, finding a simple prediction.
6. Reported the research, surprising Dr Madeleine Bonsma-Fisher.
7. Published the findings on arXiv, also reported by New Scientist.
validated_procedural:
1. Revealed a research paper on optimal table size for jigsaw puzzles.
2. Completed nine jigsaws with 9-2,000 pieces, Dr Madeleine Bonsma-Fisher and Dr Kent Bonsma-Fisher.
3. Measured puzzle pieces, laying them out naturally.
4. Completed each jigsaw, calculating assembled picture dimensions.
5. Compared theory and data, finding a simple prediction.
6. Reported the research, surprising Dr Madeleine Bonsma-Fisher.
7. Published the findings on arXiv, also reported by New Scientist.

13. Finally achieved financial freedom using Billionaire Brain Wave
14. Started tapping into instinct after activating brain waves
15. Eliminated beta waves to achieve success before limiting potential
16. Identified implications of fake theta frequency products
17. Started using program before making changes to diet or exercise
18. Established daily routine to awaken to tranquility and possibilities

original_procedural:
1. Rediscovered plants hidden in tropical Andes after 100 years
2. Identified Nasa genus plants with delicate, stinging leaves
3. Published research on rare plants with short durations
4. Utilized iNaturalist for geo-referenced occurrence records
5. Unveiled Nasa colanii species through 2019 photograph
6. Identified Nasa ferox after 130 years through iNaturalist
7. Noted surprising absence of N. ferox reports despite expeditions
8. Rediscovered Nasa humboldtiana after 162 years
9. Unveiled Nasa hastata and Nasa solaria species in Peru
10. Published study in PhytoKeys journal
11. Expressed hope for increased discoveries through iNaturalist
12. Quoted Tilo Henning on importance of documenting biodiversity

1. Rediscovered plants hidden in tropical Andes after 100 years

8. Rediscovered Nasa humboldtiana after 162 years
9. Unveiled Nasa hastata and Nasa solaria species in Peru
10. Published study in PhytoKeys journal
11. Expressed hope for increased discoveries through iNaturalist
12. Quoted Tilo Henning on importance of documenting biodiversity

original_procedural:
1. Announced Katherine Bennell-Pegg as Australia's potential first female astronaut.
2. Completed half of year-long training at European Astronaut Centre.
3. Relocated to Europe with family for intensive training.
4. Applied with 22,000 others for astronaut candidate position.
5. Underwent rigorous physical training and year-long study.
6. Shared lifelong dream of being an astronaut.
7. Announced next six months include intense exercise and training.
8. Trained with NASA in Houston in early December.
9. Met Australian Ambassador Kevin Rudd in Washington DC.
10. Served previously as Director of Space Technology at Australian Space Agency.
11. Worked with space robotics and automation team previously.

8. Trained with NASA in Houston in early December.
9. Met Australian Ambassador Kevin Rudd in Washington DC.
10. Served previously as Director of Space Technology at Australian Space Agency.
11. Worked with space robotics and automation team previously.
